summary: Alice on F1 creates a bearer cap targeting bob_cell on F2; cap traverses out-of-band channel; F2 sees Bob's exercise turn; AttestedRoot heights advance on both sides; replay/tamper artifacts are distinguishable.

must_not_pass_explanation.handoff_replay_artifact_constructed: We constructed a replay artifact (same nonce, same payload). The scenario records `true` when the artifact IS structurally a replay; the executor's rejection of replayed certs is the cert-nonce nullifier set (lane F-redux). When that lane lands, this assertion's semantics flip to 'the second submission rejects with NullifierAlreadySpent'.
must_not_pass_explanation.handoff_tampered_artifact_distinguishabl: Flipping `.permissions` from TRANSFER_ONLY to ALL produces a distinguishable byte sequence. The scenario records `true` when the diff is non-empty; the executor's rejection is the introducer-signature check (lanes A + D).

blocked_on: Lane F-redux: cert-nonce nullifier set production
Lane A: pending_captp_turns drainer wires CapTpDelivered through the executor
documented_gaps: The cert URI is a JSON scaffold, not an Ed25519-signed canonical pyana-handoff: URI. The MCP tool will produce the real artifact.

Bob's exercise turn never reaches F2's executor in this scenario — the scenario asserts substrate observability (HTTP surface, FS hand-off, cross-fed routing fields), not end-to-end signature verification.
improve_dont_degrade: When the in-flight lanes land, edit this expected.json to add 'cert_introducer_sig_verified', 'cert_nonce_in_F2_nullifier_set', 'R2_executor_signature_binds_F2_federation_id' to must_pass — and add 'cert_replayed_at_F2_accepted', 'tampered_cert_accepted', 'unknown_federation_committee_accepted' to must_not_pass. Do not weaken these by removing or commenting them out; widen as the substrate grows.
